app.title: Albion Packet Explorer
welcome.title: Welcome to Albion Packet Explorer
welcome.body: Start a live capture or open a saved JSON log to begin. New to packets? See the Glossary in Settings for plain-language definitions.
toolbar.update.tip: Show what's new and choose to update, skip or dismiss
update.dialog.title: Update available: v{0}
update.dialog.noNotes: No changelog was provided for this release.
update.dialog.skip.tip: Don't prompt for this version again
toolbar.device.tip: Select network adapter to capture Albion traffic from.
toolbar.capture.tip: Start live packet capture. Needs Npcap on Windows, or capture privileges (root/setcap) on Linux and macOS. If it fails, the app shows how to fix it.
toolbar.stop.tip: Stop live capture. All captured packets remain visible.
toolbar.pause.tip: Pause capture. Incoming packets are dropped but the session is kept; resume to keep recording into the same session.
toolbar.resume.tip: Resume capture into the same session.
toolbar.tools.tip: Merge and verify packet capture files.
toolbar.layout.tip: Switch between side-by-side and stacked layout. (F5)
toolbar.save.tip: Save the loaded or captured packets. Choose a format.
toolbar.open.tip: Open a saved capture (JSON or raw .b64).
toolbar.toggleSidebar.tip: Show or hide the filter sidebar. (F5)
sidebar.status.help:
Filter packets by kind:
EVENT = server tells the client something happened.
REQUEST = client asks the server to do something.
RESPONSE = server's reply to a request.

Click a kind to show only those packets. See the Glossary in Settings for more.
panel.eventSummary.help:
Each row = one packet type (e.g. EVENT 32).
Count = how many times seen.
Keys = which param indices were present, with % showing how often.
100% = always present (required field). Less = optional or conditional.

Click a row to filter the packet list below.
empty.noData.title: No packets yet
empty.noData.message: Start a live capture, or open a saved JSON capture, to begin exploring packets.
empty.noSelection.title: No packet selected
empty.noSelection.message: Pick a packet in the list above to inspect its fields here.
empty.byCode: Codes appear here once packets are captured or loaded.
panel.packetList.help:
All individual packets matching the current filter.
Filter is set automatically when you click a row in Event Summary.
Click a packet row to inspect its fields in the detail panel.
panel.packetDetail.help:
Key/Type/Value breakdown of the selected packet.
Key = param index (0-255). Type = wire type. Value = decoded value.
Right-click a row to edit a param name or note.
panel.packetDetail.helpShort: Right-click a row to edit a param name or note.
detach.tip: Pop this panel out into its own window
detach.dock.tip: Dock this panel back into the main window
status.ready: Select a network device and click Start, or open a file.
status.dataReady: Item data ready ({0} items, {1} icons on disk, {2}).
status.devicesFailed: Could not list devices (is Npcap installed?): {0}
status.captureNoDevice: No capture device could be opened. See the dialog to grant capture permission.
capture.help.title: Capture needs permission
capture.help.copied.title: Copied
capture.help.action.launched: Npcap installer started. Finish it, then click Start again.
capture.help.action.opened: Opened the Npcap download page in your browser.
capture.help.action.granted: Access granted. Close this and click Start.
capture.help.action.failed: Could not complete automatically. Use the steps above.
capture.help.action.daemon.ok: Done. Capture access is now restored at every boot.
capture.help.action.setup.ok: Set up. Launch Albion Packet Explorer from your menu (no password needed). The AppImage still auto-updates; re-run this after a big update.
capture.help.win.heading: Windows: install Npcap
capture.help.win.intro: Live capture needs the Npcap driver. No device could be opened, so it is likely missing or the app is not elevated.
capture.help.win.step.install: Install Npcap, then restart Albion Packet Explorer. Keep "WinPcap API-compatible mode" checked during setup.
capture.help.win.step.admin: If capture still fails after installing Npcap, run the app as Administrator (right-click the icon, then Run as administrator).
capture.help.mac.heading: macOS: grant capture access
capture.help.mac.intro: Capturing needs read access to the BPF devices (/dev/bpf*). No device could be opened, so those permissions are not granted.
capture.help.mac.step.chmodbpf: Recommended: install Wireshark and its ChmodBPF helper for persistent access, then restart the app.
capture.help.mac.step.sudo: Quick test: run the app from Terminal with sudo.
capture.help.mac.step.perms: Or open BPF permissions for this session only (resets on reboot):
capture.help.linux.heading: Linux: grant capture permission
capture.help.linux.intro: Capturing raw packets needs the CAP_NET_RAW capability. The AppImage runs unprivileged, so no device could be opened. Pick one option.
capture.help.linux.step.root: Quick test: run the AppImage as root.
capture.help.linux.step.setcap: Permanent (no sudo each launch): extract the AppImage, grant capture rights to the binary, then run it.
capture.help.linux.step.installed: If installed from a .deb or .rpm, grant rights to the installed binary instead.
capture.help.linux.step.libpcap: If it still fails, make sure libpcap is installed: Debian/Ubuntu "sudo apt install libpcap0.8", Fedora "sudo dnf install libpcap".

list.filter.edit.tip:
Click to edit filter query
Syntax: term  kind:X  code:32  name:X  params:X  paired:yes|no  failed:yes|no  returncode:N  prefix - to exclude
list.col.name: Name
detail.diff.empty: Diff compares a request with its paired response. Select a REQUEST or RESPONSE that has a matched partner.
detail.diff.legend: Green = response-only (server-added)   ·   Amber = changed   ·   Grey = request-only
diff.window.title: Packet Diff
diff.legend: Green = only in right   ·   Amber = changed   ·   Grey = only in left
detail.correlation.tip: Jump to the paired request/response packet
detail.resolved.empty: No params resolved to item names for this packet.
detail.enumLabel.tip: Your label for this value (enum-value catalog)

detail.hide.save.tip: Save current hidden rows as preset
summary.unknownFirst.tip: Sort codes with no known name to top
summary.col.name: Name
summary.refDiff.title: Reference diff
summary.refDiff.unknownCode: Unknown code: no class name to look up.
summary.refDiff.noRepo: Reference repo not found. Set APX_REFERENCE_REPO to a local checkout.
summary.gap.title: Coverage gap
summary.gap.copyUnseen.tip: Copy the list of known codes not captured this session (your coverage checklist)
summary.fields.title: Field values (selected code)
summary.fields.empty: Select a code above to see how each field's value varies (distinct count, range, most common).
fullValue.title: Full Value
editParam.badge.base.tip: Name comes from the built-in packet dictionary.
settings.title: Settings
glossary.intro: Plain-language definitions of the terms used throughout the app.
glossary.packet.def: A single message sent between the Albion game client and server. The app decodes each one into a list of fields.
glossary.event.def: A message the server pushes to the client about something that happened in the world (a mob spawned, an item changed). Most packets you explore are events.
glossary.request.def: A message the client sends to the server asking it to do something (move, use an object).
glossary.response.def: The server's reply to a specific request.
glossary.code.def: A number identifying the packet type (for example EVENT 32). All packets with the same kind and code share the same structure.
glossary.key.def: The index of a field inside a packet (0-255). Each key maps to one value. Some keys are always present; others are optional.
glossary.param.def: A single key/value pair inside a packet: the key (index), its wire type, and the decoded value.
glossary.resolve.def: Turning a raw number into a human-readable item name and icon using public Albion item data (for example index 179 becomes T1_SEAWEED - Seaweed).
glossary.schema.def: The set of names and notes you (or the built-in dictionary) attach to a packet's keys, describing what each field means.
settings.capture.title: Capture
settings.shortcuts.sidebar.desc: Show or hide the filter sidebar.
settings.shortcuts.autoSelect.desc: When on, the newest captured packet is selected automatically.
settings.shortcuts.rowExpand.desc: Expand or collapse the selected Packet Detail row (arrays and dicts).
settings.shortcuts.hint: Click a shortcut box and press a key or combination to set it. Press Esc to cancel.
settings.display.resolveNames.desc: Show UniqueName and English display name for numeric param values.
settings.display.resolveNames.note: Item data is downloaded from public Albion data sources on first launch and cached for 7 days.
settings.display.density.desc: Row height and font size for the summary, list and detail grids.
settings.display.sidebarKey.desc: Click and press a key (or combo) to set the shortcut that shows or hides the filter sidebar. Press Esc to cancel.
settings.display.tray: Close button hides to system tray
settings.display.tray.desc: Clicking X hides the window instead of closing. Use the tray icon to show or exit.
settings.display.resolveIcons.desc: Show item icons in the Packet Detail panel.
settings.display.iconMode.note: Off: no icons. Memory: fetch per session, nothing written to disk. Disk: cache to the icon folder for reuse (uses more disk).
settings.display.forceExpand.desc: Automatically expand all Packet Detail rows to show full decoded values.
settings.display.forceExpand.note: Arrays and dicts are shown one entry per line with item index resolution.
settings.display.autoStart.desc: Start network capture automatically when the app opens.
settings.display.autoStart.note: Uses the last selected device (or all adapters if set to Automatic).
settings.display.autoSave.desc: Save all captured packets to a JSON file when closing the app.
settings.display.autoSave.note: Saved to the app data logs folder with a timestamped filename.
settings.paths.appData.desc: Settings, layout, presets and the item/icon cache are stored here. Change it to move everything to a new location.
settings.paths.changeNote: Choosing a new folder moves the existing files there so nothing is left behind.
settings.paths.logFolder.desc: If you use a tool that records Albion network traffic, its packet log usually lives in one of these instance subfolders. Optional - you can also just open any saved JSON file.
settings.paths.itemCache.desc: Downloaded from public Albion data sources, refreshed every 7 days. Defaults to the App Data folder.
settings.paths.itemCache.customNote: Custom location set. The cache is normally kept inside the App Data folder; a separate folder means item data is downloaded and stored there instead.
settings.paths.iconCache.desc: Where downloaded item icons (PNGs) are stored when Item Icons is set to Disk. Point it at a folder that already holds Albion icons to reuse them.
settings.paths.findIcons.none: No existing Albion icon folders found.
toast.path.moved.title: Location Changed
toast.path.set.title: Location Changed
toast.path.failed.title: Could Not Change Location
settings.schema.desc: Export the merged param schema for an event or request as standalone JSON you can reuse.
settings.schema.preview.placeholder: Enter a kind and code above to preview the schema…
settings.theme.dark.desc: Switch between dark and light base theme.
settings.theme.color.desc: Pick a color accent for the UI.
settings.theme.language.desc: Interface language. More languages can be added by dropping a JSON file into the app's lang folder.
settings.about.name: Albion Packet Explorer
settings.about.desc: Inspect and decode Albion Online network packets from a live capture or a saved log. Explore every field of every packet to understand the game's network protocol.
